creator: bms@incunabulum.net
text:
On Mon, Jul 31, 2006 at 11:57:50PM +0200, Pav Lucistnik wrote:
> Can you paste the /var/log/messages segment? I wonder how is the probed,
> and if it creates /dev/ugen* nodes.
> 
> The gphoto devs suggest to just use umass in system...

I'm using the umass driver already. I have tried mounting it explicitly
(as /camera in fstab) but libgphoto2 still doesn't find the device.

Jul 31 18:04:34 empiric kernel: umass0: OLYMPUS C180/D435, rev 2.00/1.00, addr 2
Jul 31 18:04:34 empiric kernel: da0 at umass-sim0 bus 0 target 0 lun 0
Jul 31 18:04:34 empiric kernel: da0: <OLYMPUS C180/D435 1.00> Removable Direct A
ccess SCSI-2 device
Jul 31 18:04:34 empiric kernel: da0: 1.000MB/s transfers
Jul 31 18:04:34 empiric kernel: da0: 499MB (1023120 512 byte sectors: 64H 32S/T
499C)

I tried using ugen after your suggestion. I didn't have the ugen.ko kernel
module loaded. The kernel detects the camera as follows if I choose 'Print'
from the menu which appears, to put it into USB-PTP mode:

Aug  1 12:37:06 empiric kernel: ugen1: OLYMPUS C180/D435, rev 2.00/1.00, addr 2

gphoto2 detects it as follows:
empiric:~ % gphoto2 --auto-detect
Model                          Port
----------------------------------------------------------
Olympus C-310Z                 usb:
Olympus C-310Z                 usb:/dev/usb1,/dev/ugen1

digikam is able to auto-detect it in the same way. However, when I try
to download pictures from it, digikam says 'failed to connect to camera':

empiric:~ % gphoto2 --auto-detect --summary
Model                          Port
----------------------------------------------------------
Olympus C-310Z                 usb:
Olympus C-310Z                 usb:/dev/usb1,/dev/ugen1

*** Error ***
PTP I/O error

*** Error ***
An error occurred in the io-library ('Unspecified error'): No error description available
*** Error (-1: 'Unspecified error') ***

I get the same message(s) from gtkam also.

Hope this helps... it's strange that umass doesn't work with these gphoto2
apps as this strikes me as the easiest way to do things.

Regards,
BMS
